genre_refs: rogues-gallery / 'Worst of the Week' recurring series
description: A designed vertical 'rogues-gallery' card from ICE's recurring 'Worst of the Week' series. On a black ribbed/striped background, seven booking-style mugshot photographs of detained individuals are arranged in two rows (four across the top, three near the middle/bottom), each in a thin frame with a dark caption bar naming an alleged crime. Top row labels: 'Kidnapping', 'Murder', 'Drug Trafficking', 'Aggravated Assault'. Lower group labels: 'Child Sexual Exploitation', 'Killing Family Member', 'Larceny'. The subjects are a mix of men and women of varying apparent ethnicities. Centered between the rows is the large title in white serif: 'Worst' over 'Week', with 'of the' set in white on a red angled banner. Bottom-left is a red banner with the URL 'ice.gov/WoW'; bottom-right is the DHS seal and white 'ICE' wordmark. Style is a tabloid 'most-wanted/most-dangerous' collage. Messaging technique: criminalization and threat-amplification, presenting immigrant detainees as a gallery of violent/sexual offenders to justify enforcement; the per-tweet text explicitly weaponizes it against a U.S. senator ('these are the criminal illegal aliens you are standing with'). Target audience: general public and political opponents, to stoke fear and partisan attack.
notable_text: Worst of the Week; Kidnapping; Murder; Drug Trafficking; Aggravated Assault; Child Sexual Exploitation; Killing Family Member; Larceny; ice.gov/WoW
